warnings: Item 19 Part B monthly data table is partially cut off on page 78 (columns truncated); values extracted where legible.

extracted.brandBackground: StretchLab is a fitness studio franchise offering one-on-one and group assisted stretching services using trained Flexologists; franchises were first offered in December 2017 by a predecessor entity and the current franchisor began selling franchises in April 2023 following an internal reorganization under Xponential Fitness, Inc.
extracted.conceptRationale: StretchLab operates boutique fitness studios providing specialized assisted-stretch and flexibility services, fitting squarely within the fitness_studio category.
extracted.staffingRationale: Studios must have at least one certified Authorized Instructor (Flexologist) on-site at all times plus a manager, requiring conventional staffing.

leadership.name: Michael Nuzzo
leadership.background: CEO since August 2025; previously CEO of Eyemart Express and EVP/COO at Petco Health & Wellness.
leadership.whyItMatters: Top executive responsible for overall strategy across all Xponential brands.

leadership.name: Danielle Porto Parra
leadership.background: President since May 2026; previously Chief Brand Officer and SVP at GoTo Foods.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads brand operations and execution across Xponential's franchise portfolio.

leadership.name: Robert Julian
leadership.background: Interim CFO since March 2026; previously CFO at The RealReal and Sportsman's Warehouse.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees financial reporting and capital structure for the franchisor.

leadership.name: Gavin O'Connor
leadership.role: Chief Legal Counsel, Chief Administrative Officer, and Secretary
leadership.background: In role since November 2025; previously CAO and General Counsel at European Wax Center.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages legal risk and franchise compliance across all SPV franchisors.

leadership.name: Eric Simon
leadership.background: CDO since February 2025; previously SVP Franchise Sales & Development at The Joint Corp.
leadership.whyItMatters: Drives franchise sales and new studio development pipeline.

leadership.name: Brandon Ruiz
leadership.background: SVP Brand since December 2025; previously held director-level roles at Petco.
leadership.whyItMatters: Primary brand contact for franchisees and oversees brand standards.

item19.notes: Measurement Period is January 1 – December 31, 2025. 'Qualified Studios' operated for the full year; 448 of 486 total US studios qualified. Part A-1 reports annual Gross Revenue by quartile. Part A-2 reports Monthly Active Members. Part A-3 reports Monthly New Memberships. Part B reports monthly ramp data for 38 New Studios (soft-opened in 2025). Part C reports Monthly Membership Attrition Rate. Part D reports average revenue mix. No EBITDA or net income table is disclosed.

cohorts.label: All Qualified Studios – Annual Gross Revenue (Total)
cohorts.basis: Average annual Gross Revenue for all 448 Qualified Studios during the 2025 Measurement Period; Item 19 Part A-1, p.68.

cohorts.label: Top Quartile Qualified Studios – Annual Gross Revenue
cohorts.basis: Average annual Gross Revenue for top-quartile studios; Item 19 Part A-1, p.68.

cohorts.label: 2nd Quartile Qualified Studios – Annual Gross Revenue
cohorts.basis: Average annual Gross Revenue for 2nd-quartile studios; Item 19 Part A-1, p.68.

cohorts.label: 3rd Quartile Qualified Studios – Annual Gross Revenue
cohorts.basis: Average annual Gross Revenue for 3rd-quartile studios; Item 19 Part A-1, p.68.

cohorts.label: Bottom Quartile Qualified Studios – Annual Gross Revenue
cohorts.basis: Average annual Gross Revenue for bottom-quartile studios; Item 19 Part A-1, p.68.

cohorts.label: New Studios – Monthly Gross Revenue Ramp (Months 1–12, 2025 Soft Opens)
cohorts.basis: Average monthly Gross Revenue for studios that soft-opened in 2025, by month after opening; Item 19 Part B, p.71.

lineItems.notes: Non-refundable; $60,000 standard, reduced to $50,000 for 2nd studio or $45,000 for 3rd+ or VetFran.
Payable only if a franchise broker is owed a commission on an additional franchise purchase.
Costs for franchisee/Operating Principal to attend Initial Training Program.
Includes 3 months base rent plus 1 month security deposit estimate, plus legal/professional fees.
Includes construction, finishes, HVAC, architect, sound consultant, and permitting fees.
Permanent interior and exterior signage and graphics only.
Annual premium for minimum required insurance coverage.
Purchased from franchisor; includes stretch beds, millwork, Hyperice equipment, rollers, straps, and shipping.

lineItems.category: Pre-Sales and Soft Opening Retail Inventory Kit
lineItems.notes: Branded merchandise, apparel, and accessories for pre-sales and soft opening; includes freight.

lineItems.category: Computer System, Audio/Visual Equipment, and Related Components
lineItems.notes: POS system, tablets, wireless speakers, security cameras, and related hardware.
Minimum $15,000 required; covers pre-sales phase and first 3 months post-soft opening.
$850 per trainee for 8–10 Flexologist Training Program participants.
Covers Technology Fee ($675/mo, reduced to $150/mo during pre-sales) and Software Fee ($203/mo) for 7 months.
Working capital for first 3 months of operations and pre-sales phase expenses, net of estimated revenue.

hiddenCosts.name: Music Licensing Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must directly clear music with ASCAP, BMI, SESAC, and GMR or pay an approved vendor; amounts vary.

hiddenCosts.name: Successor Franchise Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $10,000 fee due prior to expiration of initial 10-year term to renew for one additional 10-year period.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $10,000 standard transfer fee (reduced to $750 for transfer to wholly-owned entity or $1,500 to immediate family member).

hiddenCosts.name: Relocation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $5,000 fee assessed upon submission of a proposal to relocate the studio.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Compliance Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $100 per day for each day franchisee fails to comply with material obligations under the Franchise Agreement.

hiddenCosts.name: Audit Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisor's audit costs ($500–$2,500 plus travel) payable if audit reveals ≥2% understatement of Gross Sales or franchisee failed to submit required reports.

hiddenCosts.name: Additional Training Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Up to $500 per day per trainer for additional training requested by franchisee or required on-site.

hiddenCosts.name: Alternative Supplier Approval Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $500 per day for personnel evaluating a proposed alternative supplier, plus reimbursement of travel and meals.

hiddenCosts.name: Mystery Shopper / Quality Control Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Up to $500 per year if franchisor establishes a mystery shopper or quality control program.

hiddenCosts.name: Local Advertising Requirement Minimum
hiddenCosts.description: Greater of $1,500 or 2% of prior month's Gross Sales must be spent monthly on local advertising.

hiddenCosts.name: Lost Revenue Damages
hiddenCosts.description: Upon early termination, franchisee owes net present value of up to 36 months of royalty and fund contributions based on average monthly Gross Sales.

hiddenCosts.name: Ongoing Flexologist Training Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $850 per instructor for any new instructors hired after opening who must complete the Flexologist Training Program.

operationalRisks.title: Extensive Regulatory and FTC Enforcement History
operationalRisks.description: Xponential and its brand franchisors have entered into consent orders with California DFPI, Washington DFI, Maryland, Virginia, and the FTC (paying $17M in redress) for material misrepresentations and omissions in FDDs.

operationalRisks.title: Multiple Pending Securities and Franchise Lawsuits
operationalRisks.description: XFI and affiliates face multiple pending securities class actions, shareholder derivative suits, and franchisee fraud/rescission lawsuits involving the Xponential brand portfolio.

operationalRisks.title: Short Operating History of Franchisor Entity
operationalRisks.description: Stretch Lab Franchise SPV, LLC was formed March 6, 2023 and began selling franchises April 4, 2023, giving it a limited track record as the contracting franchisor.

operationalRisks.title: Significant Number of Unopened Franchises
operationalRisks.description: As of December 31, 2025, 47 franchise agreements were signed but studios had not yet opened, indicating potential systemic delays.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Sales Quotas with Termination Risk
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must achieve trailing 12-month average monthly Gross Sales of $20,000 by year 1 and $30,000 by year 2+; failure can result in termination.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Royalty and Advertising Payments
operationalRisks.description: Royalties and brand fund contributions are owed regardless of sales levels; non-payment can result in franchise termination.

operationalRisks.title: Cross-Default Across Multiple Agreements
operationalRisks.description: A default under any one franchise or multi-unit agreement with Xponential affiliates constitutes a default under all other agreements, risking loss of all franchises.

operationalRisks.title: Supplier Control and Potential Higher Costs
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must purchase 75–91% of ongoing supplies from franchisor-designated suppliers at prices set by those suppliers, which may exceed open-market prices.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability Guarantee Required
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee's spouse must sign a guarantee making them personally liable for all financial obligations under the franchise agreement.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution in California
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be mediated and arbitrated in Irvine, California, which may disadvantage franchisees located in other states.

operationalRisks.title: System Growth Stalled in 2025
operationalRisks.description: Net franchised unit growth was only +1 in 2025 (485 to 486) versus +57 in 2024 and +145 in 2023, with 37 studios ceasing operations in 2025.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor May Modify Business Model Without Consent
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor can change the Learning Management System and System Standards at any time, potentially requiring additional franchisee investment.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p.23 note 1 states typical studio is approximately 1,100 to 1,500 sq ft; no per-sqft rent rate disclosed — Item 7 lease line is a 3-month deposit+rent estimate of $16,200–$50,000.

riskReasons: 6 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $542,615 mid-point build-out, there is a $292,615 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $32,669/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: StretchLab operates boutique fitness studios providing specialized assisted-stretch and flexibility services, fitting squarely within the fitness_studio category.

benchmark.label: Boutique fitness / studio
benchmark.dominantRisk: Member retention/churn and fixed-cost coverage — NOT cost of goods. The deal is decided by recurring membership against rent and equipment lease.
benchmark.rampNote: 9–18 months to a mature, retained member base.
considerations: COGS is trivial; the risks are member churn and covering fixed rent + equipment lease.
Ask Item 20 operators for monthly retention, average membership lifetime, and break-even member count.
Model CAC and free-trial conversion — member acquisition cost is the hidden operating line.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 15–28% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 2.5–3.7 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Boutique fitness / studio” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 12–20% category band.
Estimated at the 3–10% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 20–30% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing; positive net worth; the business is profitable; the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by Deloitte & Touche LLP): Net income of $151.66M on $227.84M of revenue — roughly 0.3x spent per $1 earned. Positive net worth of $25.53M. About 399% of liabilities is money owed to related parties — the entity is kept alive largely by insider loans, not profit. Cash of $5.92M against $14.22M of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 0.85). Note: about 98% of those liabilities is deferred revenue — prepaid franchise fees the company owes as future services, not borrowed money. Netting it out, liabilities are still about 0.0x assets.

Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. XPOF Assetco, LLC guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: more due within a year than current assets to cover it
heavy dependence on related-party loans to stay funded
elevated unit closures (37 in the last year, about 8% of the system)
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
